blocks.body: Sentence Transformations — Passive shows how to move the focus from the doer to the action or result while keeping the correct tense and passive form.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"In passive transformations, you are not just rearranging words. You need to keep the tense, aspect, modality, and focus, but move attention to the action or the result.","rules":[{"label":"OBJECT -> SUBJECT","color":"emerald","text":"In most rewrites, the object of the active sentence becomes the subject of the passive sentence.","example":"The bridge is being repaired."},{"label":"KEEP THE TENSE","color":"blue","text":"The form be + V3 must reflect the same tense: is built, was built, has been built, will be built.","example":"The meeting has been cancelled."},{"label":"BY ONLY WHEN NEEDED","color":"amber","text":"Use by + agent only when the doer matters to the meaning. Otherwise, leaving it out is often better.","example":"The novel was written by Orwell."}]}

blocks.heading: 1. How to Spot the Needed Rewrite
blocks.body:
A passive transformation is useful when the centre of attention is not the doer, but the action, the result, or the affected object.

  • First find the direct object of the active sentence. It often becomes the new subject.
  • Then identify the tense and verb form: simple, continuous, perfect, or modal structure.
  • After that, build the correct be + V3 or modal + be + V3 pattern.
  • At the end, decide whether you really need a by-phrase. If the doer is obvious or unimportant, it is better to omit it.

blocks.heading: 2. Core Transformation Patterns
blocks.body:
Base sentenceTarget sentenceRule focus
People speak English in many offices.English is spoken in many offices.Present Simple active -> passive.
They have cancelled the meeting.The meeting has been cancelled.Present Perfect passive with has been + V3.
Someone will send you the link tomorrow.The link will be sent to you tomorrow.will + be + V3; the indirect object can stay after the verb.
They are repairing the bridge.The bridge is being repaired.Continuous passive with being.

If the sentence has two objects, two passive versions may be possible, but exam tasks usually want the most natural one.

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Forgetting the verb be in the passive.","wrong":"The bridge repaired every year.","right":"The bridge is repaired every year.","hint":"Without the correct form of be, the passive does not work."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Using the second form instead of the third.","wrong":"The report was wrote yesterday.","right":"The report was written yesterday.","hint":"The passive needs the past participle (V3), not Past Simple."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Mixing up the simple and continuous passive.","wrong":"The room is cleaned right now.","right":"The room is being cleaned right now.","hint":"If the action is in progress now, you need being."},{"label":"Mistake 4","color":"rose","title":"Adding by automatically even when the agent is not important.","wrong":"The road was closed by the authorities.","right":"The road was closed.","hint":"Keep the by-phrase only when it adds real information."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick Exam Check","items":["Find the object of the active sentence and make it the new subject.","Carry the same tense and aspect into the be + V3 pattern.","Decide whether by + agent is needed or whether it only makes the sentence heavier.","Before finalising the answer, check that the meaning is still the same even though the focus has changed."]}
